rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Cloud/remote features carry a Git dependency — out of scope.
Local scheduled tasks catch up on wake.
Verify capabilities BEFORE asserting, not after.
rules_evaluated.evidence: build-your-challenge-memory.md line 64: "Options 2 (manual per-file approval) and 3 (Ultraplan on web)" — 'Ultraplan' is not in the platform compendium; cannot be verified from dated notes. The four-options dialog wording and option numbering are also behavior-specific (exact UI string) and should be live-tested.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add Ultraplan to memory/reference_claude_code_capabilities.md after a live probe; or remove the named option and refer reader to the quick reference only.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Known correct Claude Code facts (easy to get wrong): Plan mode = Shift+P (or Shift+Tab cycle).
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Shift+Tab cycle is present in both the exercise and the quick reference, consistent with the compendium. The compendium's additional Shift+P shortcut is not contradicted by the exercise omitting it — omission is fine.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — write 'start a new session in X,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
Source URL + type label — mandatory.
rules_evaluated.evidence: building-agent-systems.md line 8: "(5) Candidate: Lindenberg on Claude Code memory architecture." — practitioner named without URL or source-type label. The word 'Candidate:' also appears literally in student-facing prework, signalling an unresolved TODO.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Either supply a URL + [practitioner direct] label and remove the 'Candidate:' marker, or remove the item entirely until it can be verified.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label — mandatory (Karpathy).
rules_evaluated.evidence: building-agent-systems.md line 23: "You saw Karpathy's LLM Wiki post in the prework." — practitioner named in student-facing body without a URL or source-type label in this file. The prework file (lectures/module-2-prework.md) may carry the URL, but the reference in body here is bare.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Confirm the URL lives in module-2-prework.md with [practitioner direct] label; if yes, body reference is acceptable as a named callback and this can be downgraded to N/A.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: build-your-challenge-memory.md line 82 has `[sources/<filename>]` inside a fenced block — this is Claude's output-format instruction, not a student fill-in placeholder. Correctly classified as PASS; FP candidate if flagged.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Open-hook: student input inside ONE fenced block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: build-your-challenge-memory.md lines 175-182: Close prompt fence ends with a colon ('…citing which memory file each claim came from:'), then fence closes (```), then 'Then the question.' appears outside the fence as a step instruction. Student input (the real question) must be appended inside the same fenced block before sending — the colon is inside but the closing backticks precede the instruction, breaking the one-fence rule.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Append 'My question:' as the final line before the closing ``` so the student types directly into the block before sending.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: build-your-challenge-memory.md line 160: 'Phase 4. Let it maintain itself.' is a section header, not a verb-sentence action lead-in (under 20 words, command verb). Also line 73 (Beat 3 build-memory prompt): preceded only by the closing of the rt-cowork block with no dedicated action-verb lead-in sentence.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add a one-sentence verb-lead before each **Prompt** label: e.g. 'Ask Claude to audit the memory for contradictions, missing citations, and staleness.' for Phase 4; 'Ask Claude to build memory/ from the curated sources.' for Beat 3.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference AND deterministic path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: build-your-challenge-memory.md line 129: 'Read the agent file you just created, apply its role and rules, and use my memory.' — back-reference anchor present ('just created') but no deterministic path to agents/<filename>. The filename was set dynamically in Phase 2 prompt 1 so exact name is unknown, but 'in agents/' should be named.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add 'in agents/' after 'just created' so Claude knows which directory to look in: 'Read the agent file you just created in agents/'.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight.
Runtime-fork prompt blocks: paired divs, shared chip.
Artifact destinations are explicit subpaths, never naked filenames.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Debrief prompt names './CLAUDE.md' with ./ prefix (correct per rule 23). Exercise prompts reference 'sources/', 'memory/', 'agents/' subdirectories consistently.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism: every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path must exist.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: All linked files confirmed present: exercises/name-your-challenge.md, exercises/build-your-challenge-memory.md, lectures/first-scheduled-agent.md, lectures/compounding.md, lectures/module-2-prework.md, exercises/personal-agent-homework.md, exercises/module-3-prework.md, supplementary/agent-trigger-list.md, supplementary/what-is-an-agent.md.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: The main forcing functions (plan-mode enable, citation rule, sources-first rule) are inside fenced prompt blocks. Body prose around them is contextual framing, not the forcing function itself.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs a smoke-test before being trusted.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: No verification machinery is built in this module — memory/agents/ creation only. Rule 36 applies when Module N builds a hook/judge/eval and immediately relies on it.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers as staged progression.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Module correctly uses ./CLAUDE.md auto-load tier, memory/ as explicit-load context. No phantom auto-load claims.

platform_claims_checked.claim: Connectors let the agent combine clues that normally live apart: a wiki page, a document, a meeting note, an email thread, and a useful source from the open web.
Tell Claude 'Enable plan mode.' (Alternatives: pick Plan from the mode dropdown at the bottom of the Claude Code desktop app, or press Shift+Tab to cycle.)
Claude will pause and ask 'Claude has written up a plan and is ready to execute. Would you like to proceed?' with four options.
Options 2 (manual per-file approval) and 3 (Ultraplan on web) aren't needed here.
Plan mode exits automatically after an approved plan runs, so the footer is already back to default.
Claude Code CLI reads any path you name. For sources outside the training folder, give Claude the absolute path; it reads the file directly.

citations_checked.claim: Lindenberg on Claude Code memory architecture (prework candidate reading)
Karpathy's LLM Wiki post referenced in body
Karpathy's LLM Wiki pattern (maintainer block, not student-facing)
File-based agentic RAG (Boris Cherny / Anthropic observation) (maintainer block, not student-facing)

_notes.url_liveness: No URLs present in either building-agent-systems.md or build-your-challenge-memory.md. URL-liveness surface N/A for this pair.
_notes.skills_surface: No [Skill: <name>] references found in target file or anchor exercise. Skills surface N/A.
_notes.anchor_exercise_spot_checked: build-your-challenge-memory.md fully read and evaluated alongside module file.
fp_candidates: build-your-challenge-memory.md line 82: `[sources/<filename>]` inside fenced block — citation-format instruction to Claude, not a student fill-in placeholder. Correctly PASS under rule 1 but a naive regex check would flag it.
Karpathy body reference (line 23) — URL may live in lectures/module-2-prework.md (not checked). If it does, the body callback is an acceptable named reference and the citation finding should be N/A.
